case.paper_title: Caste as an Impediment to Trade
case.focal_claim: [the study] finds substantially higher income for low-caste households residing in villages dominated by BACs [compared to villages dominated by upper caste].

original_datasets.name: Household-level data
original_datasets.columns: [hhcode, village, domlow, totinc, cropincacre, literate, totland, buywater, ownpump, tenant, landlord, rainfall, naturalwater, dist1-dist25, caste4, caste5, bihar, bfrelsc, dlbfrelsc, dlbuywater, dlownpump, dltenant, dllandlord]

summary_statistics.info: Rows: 1666; Columns: 85; Example dtypes: float32 (66), float64 (10), category (5), int16 (2), int32 (2). Key non-null counts: totinc (1666), domlow (1295), village (1666).

original_datasets.notes: Primary dataset used for modeling total income (totinc) by village low-caste dominance (domlow). domlow is non-missing for 1295 observations. Village identifier stored as numeric.

original_datasets.name: Table 2 household summary data
original_datasets.columns: [hhcode, village, domhigh, domlow, totinc, cropinc, cropincacre, literate, totland, pricland, landirr, ownpump, buywater, tubewell, tenant, landlord, bfrelsc, tyields, cropvariety]

summary_statistics.info: Rows: 1670; Columns: 21. Key non-null counts: totinc (1670), domhigh (1297), domlow (1297), village (1670).

original_datasets.notes: Used to cross-check village dominance variables and observation counts; not the main modeling dataset.

tasks.task_instruction: Conduct the analysis without additional restrictions.

analysis_path.path_description: Import household.dta, assess normality and outliers, then fit a multilevel model of total income on low-caste village dominance with households nested in villages. Also report OLS as a robustness check.
preprocessing_steps: Import original household.dta and table2.dta from /app/data.
Merge domhigh/domlow indicators from table2.dta for cross-checks based on hhcode.
Compute skewness and kurtosis for totinc.
Compute Mahalanobis distance on [domlow, totinc] to flag potential household-level outliers (retain full data regardless).
Identify six outlier villages (IDs: 70, 61, 60, 84, 59, 58) and create a dataset excluding them.
Construct grand-mean standardized totinc for diagnostics.

key_choices.main_predictor_measure: Village-level low-caste dominance (domlow, coded 1 for low-caste dominated, 0 otherwise).
key_choices.sample_restriction: None for main; repeat models excluding six identified outlier villages (70, 61, 60, 84, 59, 58).
key_choices.outlier_rule: Flagged six village-level outliers based on random effects diagnostics; models reported with full data and excluding these villages. Household-level Mahalanobis outliers were retained.
key_choices.data_processing: Merge dominance indicators from table2.dta to household.dta; compute diagnostic metrics; subset to exclude six villages for robustness.
key_choices.model_choice: Linear mixed-effects model with random intercepts for village; OLS of totinc on domlow as robustness.
key_choices.inference_rule: Coefficient sign and p-value/CI for domlow.

main_predictor.name: domlow
main_predictor.definition: Indicator for low-caste dominated village (1) versus high-caste dominated (0).

analysis_code.notes: Python rewrite of the reported R analysis: fits MixedLM totinc ~ domlow with random intercept by village, and OLS totinc ~ domlow; computes normality and Mahalanobis diagnostics; repeats excluding six outlier villages.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Follow this planned path; do not change the focal claim, dataset, or analysis goal.

tasks.task_instruction: Do not use District controls, Crop controls, Distance controls, Groundwater controls, or Public goods controls. Do not include irrigation, credit, and tenancy variables or their interactions. Do not use instruments for water buyer status.

analysis_path.path_description: Fit OLS of total income on low-caste village dominance with and without the six outlier villages; report main result as a t-statistic and p-value.
preprocessing_steps: Ensure domlow and totinc are available; drop rows with missing values in these variables.
Create a dataset excluding the six outlier villages (70, 61, 60, 84, 59, 58).

key_choices.sample_restriction: None for main; repeat models excluding six outlier villages.
key_choices.outlier_rule: Exclude six villages identified as outliers in a robustness check.
key_choices.data_processing: No controls or instruments included per instruction.
key_choices.model_choice: OLS regression of totinc on domlow.
key_choices.inference_rule: t-statistic, p-value, and 95% CI for the domlow coefficient.

main_predictor.name: domlow
main_predictor.definition: Indicator for low-caste dominated village (1) versus high-caste dominated (0).

analysis_code.notes: Task2 mirrors Task1 under explicit restrictions: report OLS results (and MixedLM as supplementary if desired), with no additional controls or instruments.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Use the planned linear regression path without adding controls, interactions, or instruments.

files.fr167_analysis__py.py: Python script that executes Task1 and Task2: reads /app/data/AEJApp-2009-0289-data/*.dta, computes diagnostics, fits MixedLM and OLS models of totinc ~ domlow with and without six outlier villages; writes results to /app/data/results_fr167.json.

codebase.notes: Original reanalysis was in R; this is a faithful Python translation using pandas, statsmodels, and scipy. Ensure the data folder is mounted at /app/data with the AEJApp-2009-0289-data subfolder present.

analysis.instructions: Execute Task1 and Task2 using the original household.dta dataset and produce structured outputs including coefficients, standard errors, p-values, and 95% CIs for domlow under MixedLM and OLS, both with full data and excluding six outlier villages.
not_allowed_actions: replace the planned path with a different analysis path

debugging_rule.repair_scope: fix dependency, file path, or output extraction issues only; do not redesign the analysis.

analysis.comparison_metrics: Coefficient for domlow, standard error, t/z-statistic, p-value, 95% CI; model type (MixedLM/OLS); sample definition (full vs excluding six villages).
